_comment: Per-program relation edges: (program_id x entity_id) -> relation + read_through (spec/03). One entry per typed competitor. The shared FACT about an entity lives once in state/entities/<entity_id>.json; the READ-THROUGH (what that fact means for THIS program) lives here. Seeded empty: entities are typed onto the program at run time via the promotion path (config seed_competitors, then house-sweep discovery). Populating this from the v1 roster is a deferred HITL curation session, not a compile step (spec/03 'Migrating the seeded roster').

read_through.text: HER3-DXd is a target twin, not a mechanism twin: it shares HER3 with HMBD-001 but delivers a topoisomerase-I payload rather than blocking signalling, so its outcomes speak to the target's tractability and to ADC chemistry — never to whether signalling blockade works. That distinction is what makes this cycle readable. On the ADC thesis — that value accrues to the linker-payload platform owner rather than to whoever picks the target — this is confirming evidence of the uncomfortable kind: Daiichi picked the target that HMBD-001 also picked, missed with it, and responded not by defending HER3 but by reallocating to I-DXd and R-DXd, the same DXd chemistry aimed elsewhere. The platform absorbed the loss; the target ate it. For HMBD-001 the practical consequence is that the field's loudest HER3 validation event is gone and no ADC readout is scheduled to replace it, which removes both a threat and a crutch: nobody is going to prove HER3 is drug-worthy on this program's behalf before its own Phase 1b data exists. Read the launch-timing revision as a portfolio verdict on this asset, not as evidence about the HER3 axis — the EGFRm-lung miss is confounded by setting and by payload, and a signalling antibody's failure modes do not overlap with an ADC's.

This is the asset the watching-tier interest was written to catch, and it arrived through the one channel the sweep can read — an English-language journal — which is itself the argument for that interest. SDP0505 shares HER3 with HMBD-001 but couples it to c-Met and delivers a payload, so it is a target twin: its success would validate that HER3 is worth binding, not that blocking HER3 signalling does anything. It matters more than its stage suggests because it is aimed at EGFR-TKI-resistant NSCLC — the exact setting where HER3-DXd just failed — and because a 2026 China BLA would put a HER3-directed agent in front of a regulator years before HMBD-001 reaches that conversation. On the ADC thesis, it confirms: another sponsor picking HER3 and betting the differentiation on chemistry and a second antigen rather than on the target choice. The read for the decision-owner is timing, not threat — a China approval sets a global reference point for what HER3 engagement is worth, and it would land while HMBD-001's Phase 1b is still generating.

Of everything that moved this cycle, this is the only HER3 co-targeting asset with human efficacy attached, and the numbers are not marginal — 64.5% ORR in TNBC at RP2D, already carried into Phase III. Its bearing on HMBD-001 is oblique but real: it is a target twin whose HER3 arm rides alongside TROP2 and a payload, so a win reads as 'HER3 is a useful thing to bind', never as 'HER3 signalling is a useful thing to block'. That distinction is the whole reason to type it carefully rather than let a strong response rate frighten the program. On the ADC thesis it is confirming and rather pointedly so: Alphamab's differentiation is bispecific format and payload engineering, and the HER3 arm is one of two targets chosen in service of the chemistry. The genuine consequence for the decision-owner is that the field's HER3 narrative is being rewritten in breast cancer by a sponsor with no lung program, at the same moment the lung HER3 flagship stalled — so the next thing 'everyone knows' about HER3 may come from a setting HMBD-001 does not compete in, and will still set the tone in which its data is received.

AK138D1 is an anti-HER3 antibody plus a topoisomerase-I payload — which is to say, it is architecturally the same bet as HER3-DXd, restarted by a different sponsor in the same quarter that Daiichi's version lost its launch date. That timing is the finding. It is a target twin to HMBD-001: same antigen, payload delivery instead of signalling blockade, so nothing it produces will speak to HMBD-001's mechanism either way. What earns it attention is the company attached. Akeso is the sponsor the platform-vs-asset thesis names as the proof that a mid-cap with one Phase 3 curve moves Big Pharma, and it is now pairing a HER3 ADC with its own setting-rival bispecific in a single 860-patient protocol. On the ADC thesis this confirms cleanly — a new entrant picking the identical target and differentiating on payload and combination rather than on target selection. The near-term relevance to HMBD-001 is low: it is breast cancer, Phase Ib/II, China-sited, and nowhere near the squamous arena. The medium-term relevance is the combination architecture, which is a template that travels to lung if it works.
